paper_title: Extending Thomassen's conjecture to directed graphs
summary: The paper arXiv:2510.11311 introduces the notion of Eulerian-avoidable digraphs as a variant of avoidable digraphs where the host is restricted to Eulerian digraphs (equal out- and in-degree at every vertex), and poses as an open problem which digraphs are Eulerian-avoidable. The main results of the paper concern avoidable digraphs in regular and general settings but do not address the Eulerian-host restriction. No follow-up work characterizing Eulerian-avoidable digraphs was found in multiple web searches conducted in May 2026.
search_queries: Eulerian-avoidable digraph characterization Thomassen conjecture directed graphs 2025
arXiv 2510.11311 Christoph Janzer Petrova Steiner Thomassen directed graphs
avoidable digraph F-free subdigraph minimum out-degree Eulerian host 2026
notes: The term 'Eulerian-avoidable' is introduced in arXiv:2510.11311 itself and does not appear in any prior or subsequent indexed literature found. The paper proves a full characterization of avoidable digraphs among regular host digraphs, and separately raises Eulerian-avoidability as a potentially richer open variant complementing the regular-host result. No follow-up was found; open with high confidence given the recency of the paper (7 months as of review date).
